page: Microsoft Azure F64as_v7 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Fasv7 server family, built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable for external block storage attachments.

In benchmark evaluations, the F64as_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. Its AMD EPYC processor achieves top-decile results in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, as well as PassMark CPU and database operations. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with cached small-block reads reaching 11,734.58 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 385.94 GB/sec. The instance also excels in high-throughput scenarios, delivering 168.89 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 95.49 GB/sec in static web serving throughput.

The primary hardware tradeoff of the F64as_v7 is the complete absence of local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, which limits its utility for local-disk-heavy tasks or hardware-accelerated deep learning. However, its high-frequency dedicated cores and balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio offer strong qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy, non-GPU workloads. It is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, database operations, multi-threaded decompression, and CPU-based machine learning inference on small to medium models, where it maintains strong to top-tier token generation speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure F64as_v7 is a high-performance virtual machine from the Fasv7 family, powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs with a balanced 256.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks place the F64as_v7 in the top decile for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput. It also delivers exceptional memory bandwidth and high-speed Redis operations. This configuration is highly efficient for compute-intensive workloads, high-throughput web serving, database management, and CPU-based LLM inference for small to medium models.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64as_v7 is a virtual machine featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz and 256 GB of RAM. It delivers top-tier CPU and database performance for compute-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F64as_v7 is a 64-vCPU virtual machine powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processor and 256 GB of RAM, optimized for high-compute workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency sixty-four core processor platform with balanced memory, engineered for demanding database operations and high-throughput web serving.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz
256 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and static web serving benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU, requiring external Premium Storage
Optimized for high-throughput web serving, databases, and CPU-based LLM inference
